data.text: I’m checking the reachability evidence and the `internal/metric` package first, then I’ll decide symbol-by-symbol whether this is a wiring pass or an obsolete-code deletion pass. If the package needs broader changes than the bead allows, I’ll stop and write the required no-changes rationale instead of half-fixing it.

data.text: I’ve confirmed the dead symbols are all clustered in `cli/internal/metric`, so this is likely a wiring/deletion decision within one subsystem rather than a broad cross-package split. Next I’m tracing current call sites from `cmd` and the metric tests to see whether the runtime path already exists and the deadcode report is a false positive or whether some methods are genuinely orphaned.

input.command: /bin/zsh -lc "rg -n \"NewStore\\(|\\.Run\\(|\\.Compare\\(|\\.Validate\\(|\\.LoadDefinition\\(|\\.History\\(|\\.Trend\\(|\\.GroupedHistory\\(|\\.ListArtifacts\\(\" cli/cmd cli/internal -S && printf '\\n==== metric tests ====\\n' && sed -n '1,420p' cli/internal/metric/store_test.go && printf '\\n==== cmd references ====\\n' && rg -n \"metric\" cli/cmd -S"

/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' cli/cmd/metric.go && printf '\\n====\\n' && sed -n '1,260p' cli/cmd/command_factory.go && printf '\\n====\\n' && sed -n '1,240p' cli/cmd/doc.go && printf '\\n====\\n' && sed -n '1,260p' cli/cmd/metric_acceptance_test.go"

/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' cli/main.go && printf '\\n==== root command refs ====\\n' && rg -n \"newMetricCommand\\(|metricStore\\(|LoadDefinition\\(|GroupedHistory\\(|Trend\\(|Compare\\(|Validate\\(\" cli/cmd -S"

data.text: The CLI does wire `internal/metric` into the production command tree, so the deadcode output is probably pointing at a narrower issue: some methods are reachable only through the package’s own command handlers, while deadcode sees their implementations as still orphaned or internally uncalled. I’m verifying that by running the production reachability tool and then tracing the exact `exec` bridge path before I touch anything.
